item.title: Alan Cumming from the show "Tip Toe" - pronouns and gender identity have always been a conservative problem.
item.description: I adore him, and as someone who isn't generally sexually attracted to men, his version of the Caberet host on Broadway is why I went from saying "not sexually attracted to men" to "not USUALLY attracted to men." ? FP EDIT: shit. I just thought a few people might feel a little more seen and enjoy the clip. Happy pride month everyone! And always remember, Stonewall was a riot. Be brave, love, and love yourselves!

item.title: They Voted No.
item.description: From page edit: Few of you are getting two states confused because there was another post earlier about a singular individual who voted no in Minnesota. Although this other state, Missouri, starts with the letter M, I assure you they're drastically different places. You know why. Michael Davis (R) Ben Whaley (R) Bryant Wolfin (R)

item.title: what they took from us all
item.description: After 7 months in detention, Olga lost her case. A judge granted her 1 month to leave the country, a detention officer informed that Olga would be sent directly to Guatemala instead. One meeting was arranged quickly for her children to meet her there. For 2 weeks, the children despaired of ever seeing their mother freed. Then, on Monday, Olga called to say she was being released. Once again, the chaos of the immigration system was evident. She was given no other information. Though happy to return to Lake Worth, she was filled with fear. Olga’s story is the story of the Greyhound station attendant who allowed Olga to use her phone to call her family. And stayed on the phone while a Lyft was ordered to make sure Olga got into the right car. Olga’s story is the story of the Cuban driver who kept telling Olga, “no llores, mami, yo me quedo contigo hasta que entres a la casa.” Who let her use his phone and stayed with her until she was received by the Airbnb host. Olga’s story is the story of that Airbnb host, a Mexican doctor who had done her internship among the Maya in Chiapas. Who offered to buy Olga food and clothing. Who on hearing that Olga had no phone, lent her own so that Olga could call her family through the night if her fears became too large. Olga’s story is the story of the Commissioner McVoy who immediately said yes when asked to go to Phoenix and help fly Olga home. Who drove at dawn to Miami to catch the earliest flight. Who after putting in an exhausting day said, “Olga needed to talk and I was there to listen.”
item.dc:creator: onushka

item.title: Dog's happy, man’s getting exercise, win win
item.dc:creator: kenistod

item.title: Millennial tells off Boomer and Gene X parents
item.description: Fp: Realizing that my surviving parent that I have not spoken to 10 years this month because they decided they wanted to try Stolen Valor on as their personality when two former stepbrothers and myself actively served, Couldn't even muster the bare minimum. Too frequently, we moved. Every year. He called himself a job shopper who was a A mechanical engineer that seemingly stole from anybody he could. My mother got cancer while we were in the middle of nowhere, Tennessee, and it were church groups that supported myself. Hell, I remember one time the sole of my shoe separated from my shoe, and the principal of the school themselves took me to the house to realize the bad situation we were in, and then took me to a discount shoe store and bought me a pair of shoes. This is the story of Gen X. They talk about how they had it so rough, but were the worst parents and the mentality of tough it out, I don't care, doesn't work as parenting. The same person abandoned my sick mother for an instant family. Abandoned me with her. He found companionship that he could leech off of in a younger boomer, and the cycle repeated. I'm not saying millennials or any other generation is gonna be better or is better. I am pointing out some of the inherent flaws of the generation that says they raised me, but were absent. Because they were part of a hustle culture, whatever gimmick that they were buying into at the time. And this is not a generalization. There are, of course, awesome Gen X people. This is just the experience many of us are reporting, largely, and are tired of being ignored. We have to walk uphill both ways, beside AI data farms in the sun when temperatures are deadly. I would have much rather have dealt with walking in the snow. It's not just the Boomers. Gen X has been in positions of leadership for a few decades now. This video is a perfect dissection of this mentality.
item.dc:creator: SquaresUnfiltered

item.dc:creator: LitterboxComics

item.title: “Several real women” is such a fun way to say “I made this up in my head.”
item.description:
So let's talk about this for a second: One thing you'll notice in conversations about trans women specifically is the mysterious appearance of "several real women." They're everywhere. "Several real women told me..." "Every woman I know thinks..." "All the women I've spoken to agree..." Somehow these women are never actually here. They never show up in the comments. They never speak for themselves. They exist exclusively as a ventriloquist dummy for whatever opinion the person already had... The funny thing is that actual women are not a hive mind. Some women support trans women. Some women don't. Most women are busy trying to survive late-stage capitalism and aren't spending their afternoons conducting focus groups about my existence... And that's part of why I do what I do. When I started talking publicly about being trans, I quickly realized that a lot of conversations about us happen without us. People debate what we think, what we feel, what we believe, and what our lives are supposedly like... So I figured I'd just show up and speak for myself. You don't have to agree with me. You don't have to find me convincing. But I think conversations get a lot healthier when we're talking to actual trans people instead of imaginary groups of "real women," "normal people," or whatever other crowd gets summoned to support an argument... If someone has a criticism of me, they can make it themselves. Dave doesn't need to bring twelve invisible women to court as character witnesses.
Anyway, here is the video that scared Dave and those "women" so much...
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.title: My cancer seems to like Éstrogen
item.description: FML, right? I'd held off doing my weekly shot for three weeks, in the end I just couldn't stand it. Even knowing that it might shorten my life I did my shot — it's that important to me. That vital. This morning my lump is noticeably larger. Still I'd rather live as me than merely exist. One week until my oncology appointment. Fingers crossed they can just cut it out.
item.dc:creator: ThatOtherGirlYouKnow

item.title: This could potentially really suck...
item.description:
The Pocket Fire flared up yesterday afternoon around 4:30 PM. I happened to be doing a jeep tour at the time and got these photos. Edit: in case you didn't know whereabouts I'm from, this is near Sedona, Arizona. Apologies, I guess I kinda of assumed everyone knew where I was from, please forgive me.
Last I checked (and I haven't yet this morning, having JUST woke up and edited these pictures), it was just over 100 acres.
Somewhere near Secret Canyon and Vultee arch. Those flames you see are somewhere around 30-60 feet high.
Oak creek canyon and the campers and people living there are being evacuated and at least one jeep trail, the Vultee Arch OHV has been shut down as of 8pm last night.
I did watch a steady stream of slurry bombers and water carrier planes headed there from as far as Prescott, so we'll see what happens... Standard Daily Meme post coming shortly.
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1723 (suspectrum)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
@phuckedinphilly
This is a thousand year old door which is pretty nifty.
As I've said before... I love a good Taiping Rebellion meme.
My spiritual tamagotchi is a Woodhouse's Scrub-jay.
I never find cool shirts when I go thrifting.
That's... what i do.
As someone who studied a lot of Eastern Orthodox theology... yeah, this is a big problem with much Protestant theology.
Pippin tax (and wizard tax) Today's message from Pippin: "Pippin. Glory Pippin." ___ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Let's send food to Imgurians in need
item.description:
Happy Saturday everyone, we're all excited for TheFreePizzaDude post today! If you'd like to help a struggling Imgurian get groceries or a meal today there are 2 easy ways -
You can find a request for food in the comments, reply to them and then send a DM to get an order set up Or you can contribute to the Imgurian Meal Fund and we'll send the food for you!
Anonymous Angels have been a big help in feeding more people, we love you so much! https://www.paypal.me/TheFreePizzaDude https://ko-fi.com/thefreepizzadude
And here's how you'd send it yourself! Any questions as an Angel? Just shoot us a message! We'll be on this username until 6pm central
Kevin and his tongue are here to show you how it works for requests -
It's much easier for us to help requests in the US but we'll do our best to pick up requests from any country. Putting your country in your comment helps us match you to an Angel in your area
Any questions as a requestor? Just leave a comment! We'll have helpers on the post for 6 hours
6 more hours to help as many people as we can Let's get it going! ... Today's Meal Tally 1st, 2nd, 3rd and 4th by @OP 5th by Pizza Angel @NeelyAdkins 6th, 7th, 8th and 9th by @OP 10th, 11th, 12th and 13th by @OP 14th, 15th, 16th, 17th and 18th by @OP 19th, 20th, 21st, 22nd and Michael Jordan by @OP Kobe Bryant and 25th by Pizza Angel @Jaenen 26th, 27th and 28th by Pizza Angel @Jaenen
item.dc:creator: TheFreePizzaDude
